title: AWS Cloud Practitioner — Mission 1: The Launchpad
description: Your first day at Meridian Orbital. The launch telemetry platform is down and the fundamentals of AWS global infrastructure are the only way back up.

nova.name: Nova Reyes
nova.description: Seen every outage since the founding. Unimpressed by guesswork.

keycard.name: Server Room Keycard
keycard.description: Opens the cage on sublevel two.

runbook.name: Incident Runbook
runbook.description: Meridian's dog-eared guide to not making things worse.

elements.text: You picked a day to start. Telemetry is dark across two regions and the launch window closes in six hours.
I may have... deployed everything to one Availability Zone. In one region. Is that bad?

elements.prompt: Nova looks at you. Your call, rookie.

options.text: "Before we touch anything — let's confirm how AWS regions and AZs actually relate."
"No time for theory. Start rebooting instances until something works."

elements.text: Good instinct grabbing the runbook. Page one: know your geography.
Rebooting blind. Bold. The runbook you didn't take says otherwise.

config.question: Kit deployed to a single Availability Zone. What is an Availability Zone?

options.text: One or more discrete data centers within an AWS Region
A synonym for an AWS Region
A billing boundary for AWS accounts
An edge location used by CloudFront

config.explanation: AZs are isolated data-center clusters inside a Region — deploying to several gives fault tolerance.

config.prompt: Order these from largest to smallest scope.
Nova: "Type the region code for N. Virginia — from memory."

elements.text: Multi-AZ rollout is live. Telemetry's green. Not bad for a first day.
We got there. Messily. Read the runbook before the next window.

elements.prompt: The launch clock resets. What now?

options.text: Walk Kit through the multi-AZ architecture so it never happens again.
Log off. Six hours of adrenaline is enough.

elements.text: Regions, AZs, data centers. Got it. I'm framing this diagram.

ending.title: First Day Hero
ending.description: Fixed the outage and left the team stronger than you found it.

ending.title: Launch Window Saved
ending.description: The rockets fly. The lessons can wait until tomorrow.
